courses.title: Survey of Western Art History: Prehistory Through the Middle Ages
Survey of Western Art History: Renaissance to Contemporary
Survey of Art History: Modernism Through Contemporary

notes: The Art History major combines an interdisciplinary and intercultural approach to art history of all periods and places. By thinking across current categories and boundaries and critically interrogating art history itself, students are encouraged to question the canon, to rethink the relationships between margins and centers, and to practice a socially and politically responsible art history. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. To be considered for this major, you must complete a minimum of two major preparatory courses , by the end of spring before transfer: It is STRONGLY RECOMMENDED to complete additional major preparatory courses. All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://arthistory.ucla.edu and https:// admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation courses for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.

While the Art history department does not require language beyond the College requirement, Art History majors are encouraged to study foreign language beyond what is required by the College. Students should save syllabi for all art history and art courses to petition the department for exemption from lower division requirements after admission.
